description: Add missing teardown() documentation section to the lifecycle hooks reference page.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add teardown() section to lifecycle-hooks.mdx
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want teardown() documented on the lifecycle hooks reference page so that I can find cleanup documentation in the obvious location.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx has a dedicated `## teardown()` section between `onDeactivate()` and `onNavigate()` (or after `onToolInvocationEnd()` — place it logically near `onDeactivate()` since both handle cleanup)
The section documents the method signature: `teardown?(): void;`
The section explains that teardown() is called by the platform before re-injection (plugin.update) to allow cleanup of event listeners, timers, or global state set up by the previous adapter version
The section explains the ordering: onDeactivate fires before teardown when both are defined
The section includes a code example showing a plugin that implements teardown() to clean up global state (e.g., removing a window property or clearing an interval)
The section notes that teardown() is optional — plugins that do not set up persistent side effects can omit it
The section notes that teardown() implementations must use the `override` keyword (consistent with the page intro about noImplicitOverride)
The page frontmatter `description` field is updated to include teardown in the list: 'onActivate, onDeactivate, teardown, onNavigate, onToolInvocationStart, onToolInvocationEnd'
The Hook Lifecycle table includes a row for teardown() showing it fires after onDeactivate during adapter removal/re-injection
The Full Example at the bottom of the page includes a teardown() implementation
userStories.notes:
The file is docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx (280 lines). The teardown() method is defined on the OpenTabsPlugin base class in platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts at the `teardown?(): void;` declaration. Its JSDoc says: 'Called by the platform before re-injection (plugin.update) to allow cleanup of event listeners, timers, or global state set up by the previous adapter version. Optional — plugins that do not set up persistent side effects can omit this.'

The existing page already mentions teardown incidentally in three places: (1) the lifecycle table row for 'Removal' says 'Fires before teardown()', (2) the onDeactivate section says 'either before teardown() on plugin update', (3) line 101 says 'When both onDeactivate and teardown are defined, onDeactivate fires first.' But there is no dedicated section explaining teardown itself.

For reference, the plugin-class.mdx page (docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx) already documents teardown() in its own section — use that as a reference for the description, but adapt it to the lifecycle-hooks.mdx style which includes a TypeScript signature block, a parameter table (teardown has none), a code example, and contextual explanation.

The lifecycle table currently has 5 rows (Registration/onActivate, Navigation/onNavigate, Tool start/onToolInvocationStart, Tool end/onToolInvocationEnd, Removal/onDeactivate). Add a 6th row for teardown between Removal/onDeactivate and the end — or restructure the Removal phase to show both onDeactivate and teardown in the correct firing order.

IMPORTANT: Follow the existing page's conventions exactly — all code examples use `override` keyword, all method signatures show the optional `?` marker, the page uses fumadocs Table components (not markdown tables), and all hooks are shown as methods on a class extending OpenTabsPlugin.
